title: LE-series repair droid

{{Quote|These things can install cargo, repair subsystems, pilot the ship and even communicate the spaceport personnel. Worth every credit.|Describing the LE-series|<ref name="Galactic Hunters">{{SWGTCG|set=Galactic Hunters|cardname=LE Repair Droid}}</ref>}}
The '''LE-series repair droid''', manufactured by [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]], was an amalgamation of qualities from [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droids]] and [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]].

==History==
Although LE-series [[Droid/Legends|droids]] were about four times more expensive than simple repair droids such as the [[WED Treadwell repair droid/Legends|WED Treadwell repair droid]], they were much more universal and easier to communicate with than other models of repair droid.<ref name="EGD">''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>

[[File:LErepairdroid-SWGTCGGH.jpg|thumb|left|200px|An LE-series repair droid at work]]
It could not only repair [[Starship/Legends|starships]] and devices, but also modify these and was an ideal walking tool for [[Starship/Legends|starship]] or [[Spaceport/Legends|stardock]] maintenance. The droid was also able to install the cargo in cargo holds, help with docking operation or pilot [[Swoop/Legends|swoop bikes]], [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeders]], or [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]]. With the proper programming, it could skillfully control even a medium-sized starship.
The protocol abilities allowed LE-series droids to establish contact with [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] personnel and find the best way to avoid any unnecessary bureaucracy.

The droid itself had specialized [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] and scopes, a broadband antenna, and a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] recorder and projector. It also knew a large variety of tools, starships and other vehicle types and huge amount of parts suitable for them. The skeletal body composition allowed LE-series droids to squeeze inside even the tightest places in almost every type of machinery.

The LE-series droid was very popular among spaceport crew members (as a part of staff) and [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] (as co-pilots and counterparts). Many owners gave their LE-series droids names based on their serial numbers.
